criteria.label: State the universal approximation theorem and distinguish existence from learning.
criteria.example: Write: 'One hidden layer + non-linear activation + enough units ⇒ can approximate continuous f* on compact set; does not guarantee SGD finds weights or generalizes.'

criteria.label: List at least three things UAT does not guarantee.
criteria.example: Name generalization, data/sample efficiency, and optimization success — e.g. 'UAT silent on val error and training time.'

criteria.label: Explain width vs depth using UAT and depth-separation intuition.
criteria.example: Contrast: 'UAT: wide shallow OK in theory; depth may need fewer params for same function class — pick by val loss.'

criteria.label: Diagnose a scenario where UAT applies but generalization fails.
criteria.example: Given train MSE 0.001, val MSE 1.2 on a 2048-unit MLP with 300 rows — label overfitting, recommend dropout or more data, not 'network too small.'

criteria.label: Explain why removing hidden non-linearities breaks universal approximation.
criteria.example: Show nn.Linear→nn.Linear equals one linear map; cannot fit XOR or y=sin(x) beyond linear regression.
